report_name: Hyderabad Police Warn of WhatsApp Impersonation Fraud Leading to Major Corporate Losses

threat_actors.name: WhatsApp Impersonation Fraudsters
threat_actors.description: Unnamed cybercriminals targeting corporations in Hyderabad, India, by hijacking WhatsApp Web sessions of senior executives to conduct Business Email Compromise (BEC) style fraud and instruct fraudulent financial transfers.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack is initiated with a phishing email containing a malicious link, which, when clicked by an employee, installs remote access malware.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: A Remote Access Trojan (RAT) is installed on the victim's computer after the initial phishing link is clicked, providing attackers with a foothold and complete remote control.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use social engineering through the hijacked WhatsApp account to send urgent, fraudulent financial transfer requests, a form of Business Email Compromise (BEC).

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: The attackers impersonate a senior executive, such as a CEO or CFO, using their legitimate, hijacked WhatsApp account to lend credibility to fraudulent fund transfer requests.

tools.name: WhatsApp
tools.description: The attackers hijack an active WhatsApp Web session on a compromised computer to impersonate senior executives and send fraudulent payment instructions to finance department staff.

identities.name: Corporate Entities in Hyderabad
identities.description: Corporations in Hyderabad, India, targeted by a WhatsApp impersonation fraud scheme, leading to significant financial losses.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used to host malicious links sent in initial access phishing emails to corporate employees.

infrastructure.name: RAT C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the Remote Access Trojan to maintain control over the compromised computer and monitor for active WhatsApp Web sessions.

indicators.name: Suspicious Persistent Outbound Connection from WhatsApp
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Persistent Outbound Connection from WhatsApp
id: 2b1c8f9a-e3d4-4b5c-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects WhatsApp.exe making a persistent outbound connection to an IP address not associated with WhatsApp/Meta services, which could indicate a hijacked session being controlled by a RAT.
references:
  - https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/hyderabad/hyderabad-alert-over-whatsapp-impersonation-scam-targeting-ceos-cfos-companies-lose-crores/articleshow/108993802.cms
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\WhatsApp.exe'
    Initiated: true
  filter_whatsapp_ips:
    DestinationIp:
      - '157.240.0.0/16'
      - '31.13.64.0/18'
      - '173.252.64.0/18'
      - '185.60.216.0/22'
  condition: selection and not filter_whatsapp_ips
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate connections to other services via WhatsApp if it acts as a proxy for certain content.
indicators.description: Detects network traffic from the WhatsApp desktop client to IP addresses not associated with Meta/WhatsApp, which could indicate a RAT is controlling the session.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Persistent Outbound Connection from WhatsApp
